text: It is not clear whether Sosthenes was a Christian who was beaten by the Jews or, perhaps more probably, a Jew who was beaten by the Gentiles. The same name appears in I Cor. 1.1.
This may refer either to the countries of Phrygia and Galatia, or to the district which could be indifferently described as Phrygian or Galatian (see p. 165, note 3).
‘The way of the Lord’ is perhaps not to be understood here of the Christian religion, but of that way which was prepared for the Lord by St John the Baptist (Mt. 3.3).
